question: A team relies on `camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.stream-enabled=true` to opt every worker into job streaming. Which single rename preserves the same behaviour in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Remove it entirely — job streaming is on by default in 8.8 and is no longer configurable.
Rename it to `camunda.client.stream-enabled` at the top level.

a.text: The deprecation table maps `camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.stream-enabled` directly to `camunda.client.worker.defaults.stream-enabled`. The opt-in streaming feature flag is still configured per worker default.

b.text: The entire `camunda.client.zeebe` namespace is deprecated. The stream-enabled flag does not live there in 8.8.

c.text: Job streaming is still described as an opt-in feature flag in the docs; it is not silently enabled for every worker.

d.text: There is no top-level `camunda.client.stream-enabled` property; the setting is scoped to worker defaults, with per-job-type overrides under `worker.override.<job-type>`.

explanation: All worker default properties from the deprecated `camunda.client.zeebe.defaults.*` namespace move to `camunda.client.worker.defaults.*` with the same leaf names. `stream-enabled` remains an opt-in flag that pairs polling with a long-lived stream so the broker eagerly pushes new jobs.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot Starter - properties reference
